Config comment states default FALSE is 'operator-approved in the design doc' (docs/DESIGN/SHORT_SIDE_LANE.md): the lane stays dark until the P1 paper soak proves the full sell-to-open→cover lifecycle. CC_REPORT 2026-07-12_weekend-hermetic-shortlane.md: 'P1 nearly complete (PRs #915/#916/#918), gated OFF until P2'. This is a documented staged rollout the operator himself approved, not author caution.
risk_if_on: Turns on a lane whose cover lifecycle is unproven (P1 not finished); shorts carry asymmetric squeeze risk — a low-float squeeze into a halt-UP can gap against a short catastrophically (the documented kill scenario). Finish P1 and run the paper soak (P2) first.

what_it_does: GAP-A smart post-entry hold (live_runner.py:31719): replaces the fixed 0.001 fast-bail wick buffer with a vol-adaptive band (k*rv_live*sqrt(N)) plus flow/volume/time-floor CUT gating on the first post-entry window. INVARIANT-A: only tightens/suppresses the early level-retest fast-bail; structural stop + #769 max-loss circuit run ungated above it.
why: Introduced ce1975a 2026-06-29 ('4-lever Ross chain — kill-switched') and never enabled. Since then the SAME fast-bail zone got live overlapping changes: the GATE-2 explosive lock-in floor (FCUV +21% after a 4.5s bail) and the 07-09 noise-floor clamp + leader-ignition-bypass pair, where replay proved the clamp ALONE was WORSE (pair required) — direct evidence this exact exit zone is interaction-sensitive. The 07-12 hermetic replay instrument (cent-exact) exists precisely to A/B it.
risk_if_on: Unmeasured interaction with the now-live lock-in/noise-floor stack in the winner-killer zone (premature early bails were THE loss driver); could double-suppress or fight the deployed clamp. One hermetic A/B run settles it — flip only on a green run-R.

what_it_does: INVERTED kill-switch (live_runner.py:36170): OFF (default) = the fixed wall-clock stop-out re-entry timer is REMOVED — the session recycles to WATCHING immediately and re-entry quality is enforced by reentry_escalation_decision (structure+HWM reclaim+tape buyers) plus the stopout-cycle cap. ON restores the LEGACY fixed timer.
why: dfc0f1e 2026-07-04 'REMOVE the fixed stop-out cooldown timer (default OFF; kill-switch)'. In-code evidence (live_runner.py:36162-36169): the timer was a band-aid over an old substring-classifier bug (IPW -$78.62 3s re-arm, since fixed sign-authoritative in risk_policy) and it BLOCKED the fast Ross re-buy of a strong leader — accurate-FSM CELZ 06-30 replay: 300s timer → -$108 vs 5s → +$229. OFF is the improved, replay-proven state; the flag exists only as a rollback lever.
risk_if_on: Restores the legacy clock: re-blocks fast leader re-buys on shallow pullbacks (CELZ delta ≈ $337/session on one name), regressing the 07-04 fix while adding no protection the escalation gates + stopout-cycle cap don't already provide.

what_it_does: LOCATE #1 scalp time-stop (live_runner.py:31961-31986): an ENTERED, not-green position whose deployed cadence read (le['cadence_cls']) is SLOW_CHOPPER and held >= 5 min is bailed via the existing bailout machinery; FAST runners are never time-stopped. Protective-only — can only exit sooner, never widens a stop.
why: Introduced b0d5739 2026-06-26 (LOCATE cluster B); it was NOT in the 06-27 harden→test→ON enablement batch (CC_REPORT 2026-06-27_off-items-enablement) and no replay verdict exists. Counter-signal: the cadence classifier in this exact area has a known bug history — G4 grind-hold was INERT from a cadence mismatch (activation ==FAST vs maintenance NOT SLOW_CHOPPER), and G4 exists because slow grinds are worth HOLDING; exit-family flips have burned the lane before (exit_ladder_live rolled back #855).
risk_if_on: A grind misclassified as SLOW_CHOPPER gets time-stopped minutes before its leg (grind→vertical class, the 7-17R tail the Ross exit research says pays for everything). Cheap to settle: one hermetic replay A/B + a spot-check that cadence_cls labels grinds correctly; guards (not-green only, UNCERTAIN→no-op) make it likely safe after that.

what_it_does: LOCATE #4 new dip-family LONG trigger (entry_gates.py:6215): a sharp undercut BELOW VWAP that fails to make a new low then reclaims = bear-trap/short-cover long; entry = reclaim bar high, stop = trap low. Carries all chase-guards (tape REQUIRED fail-closed, extension, backside/front-side, L2 veto) + downstream vetoes; OFF = returns disabled before any compute.
why: Introduced b0d5739 2026-06-26 (LOCATE cluster). It is part of the dip-entry family (tight_false_break_reclaim, ask_thins_dip, sub_vwap_trap, pulling_away_roc, premarket_pivot_macd + first_dip policy) whose A/B replay is running TONIGHT on this very branch (fix/tight-false-break-wire) — the evidence is in flight; decide on the A/B result, hours away.
risk_if_on: Flipping mid-A/B contaminates the baseline the replay is measuring. Substantively, a false trap-reclaim on a genuinely backside name is a knife-catch (mitigated by the guard set but exactly what the A/B quantifies).

what_it_does: NEW-INITIATION-ONLY time-of-day schedule: a bounded-upward prime-window (04:00-10:30 ET) size multiplier composed under the hard 3x clamp (live_runner.py:27704) plus a fade-driven late-day (>=14:30 ET AND regime-poor) fresh-arm suppression in auto_arm (Guard 7, auto_arm.py:4593). Never touches exits.
why: Introduced aa603e4 2026-06-27 (Punch-list P5), never enabled — and since superseded on both halves: (1) the ADAPTIVE per-ET-hour expectancy risk curve chili_momentum_time_of_day_risk_enabled is default ON ('2026-07-10, greenlit #1... no dark flags', config.py:9862) and covers the size lever data-driven instead of a fixed magic window; (2) the suppression half reuses the SAME _regime_is_poor signal as the live pre-arm move-exhaustion abandon (B1, ON since 06-27). Fixed-window boost = the magic-number version of a lever already ON adaptively.

what_it_does: GAP-1 additive entry trigger (entry_gates.py:5217): converging-wedge apex break — 3+ taps on a descending upper AND ascending lower trendline, fire on the body/wick breaking out at the apex with tape; stop = back into the wedge; rising wedges are skipped. Carries the full chase-guard set (tape REQUIRED fail-closed via tape_confirms_hold, _hod_extension_ok, backside/front-side, _l2_entry_veto) and joins the same setup-selector + LIVE_PENDING_ENTRY vetoes; OFF = disabled before any compute.

The 06-26 cup-and-handle hardening report treats wedge_break as the guard-complete REFERENCE implementation ('mirroring wedge exactly'; wedge 'already requests' the ema/macd arrays others silently missed), and its sibling from the same audit (cup_and_handle) was hardened, 22-tests-greened, and flipped ON on 06-27 (R4). No replay or live counter-evidence exists anywhere in docs — the only reason OFF is the batch's default-OFF authoring convention, which is exactly the operator's dark-flag anti-pattern. Additive entry + own guards + instant kill-switch → standing rule applies.
risk_if_on: More entry surface on coiling low-floats: a failed apex break stops out back inside the wedge (bounded by the structural stop + #769 circuit). Reversible instantly via the kill-switch; watch the first sessions' wedge-tagged fills.

what_it_does: HVM101 #008 quality filter (entry_gates.py:4808-4826) on the ALREADY-LIVE wick-reclaim entry (chili_momentum_wick_reclaim_entry_enabled default True, config.py:2881): rejects reclaim recoveries that took too many bars — 1-3 bars fire, the 4th only with drying-up proof (flush_recedes), 5-6+ rejected as a slow trickle. why: Introduced dd82cfb 2026-06-27 'wick-reclaim slow-recovery gate (Pass-4 faithfulness fix) + 9 tests'. The Part-B 3rd-pass audit punch-list explicitly flagged wick_reclaim as 'lighter-guarded than primary triggers' — this gate IS the tightening, a direct Ross-course faithfulness rule with adaptive boundary relaxation and unit tests. No counter-evidence; pure author-caution OFF on an additive veto with its own guards → standing rule.
risk_if_on: Theoretical E1-class over-veto (rejecting a slow reclaim that would have won), but scoped only to >=4-bar trickles the course itself calls invalid ('confirms the rejection, NOT a reclaim'), on one trigger, with byte-identical rollback.

what_it_does: E2 win-cycle fatigue (auto_arm.py:3054/4476, live_runner.py:27567), entries-only: today's clean-win count >= 4 (YELLOW) down-sizes NEW entries to the 0.5 fraction; >= 7 (RED) halts fresh arms entirely. Never touches an open position or exit.

Its direction contradicts the measured evidence base: the day-cushion risk ladder (#664) deliberately sizes UP with cushion (Ross's actual practice), and the lane's documented catastrophes were sizing-INVERSION/starvation on hot names — CLRO 07-02 budget starvation of the #1, and the 07-07 exp_mult-0.5 death-spiral (26sh vs 1,026sh). The give-back problem that motivates fatigue rules was root-caused elsewhere (BJDX/LUCY orphaned-fill sessions, fixed #854).
risk_if_on: Caps the right tail: halves size after the 4th win and halts after the 7th on precisely the hot streak days that must carry the $1k/day goal — re-creating the death-spiral inversion (small on the best days) that already cost the lane once.

what_it_does: Pattern-tier drawdown breaker (portfolio_risk.py:1943, check_drawdown_breaker): computes a Gaussian lower bound on 30-day realized PnL from the trailing 180d of CHILI-attributed history and trips when actual 30d PnL falls below it; a trip blocks pattern-attributed trades until manual reset (absorbing state).
why: Config comment (config.py:12622) is explicit: 'Default OFF until walk-forward shows it would have tripped on/around 2026-04-22 (the cumulative-PnL trough date from the 2026-05-15 audit)'. Renamed/separated d6ddbe8 2026-05-16 (CC_REPORT 2026-05-16_f-portfolio-vs-pattern-breaker-separation.md), and a purpose-built validation script ships with it (walkforward_monthly_dd_breaker.py). The documented acceptance test (the walk-forward) has no recorded green result.
risk_if_on: Uncalibrated K/lookback either false-trips — freezing pattern trading in an absorbing state needing manual reset — or never trips (false comfort). Run the shipped walk-forward against the 2026-04-22 trough first; that is the flag's own documented turn-on condition. (No DB queries tonight — replays are running — so this cannot be validated in this session.)

Config comment (config.py:10954-10958) carries the counter-evidence: Codex's 2026-04-27 audit found 'near-total absence of evidence on the legacy promoted-status set, so naive auto-demote would stop a lot of live trading at once'; the 2026-04-28 incident (32/34 entries on 'challenged' patterns) shows lifecycle demotion directly gates the AutoTrader entry path. what_it_does: Accounting semantics toggle (governance.py:1215): when True, manual (broker_source='manual') Trade rows count against the Robinhood per-broker daily budget/loss cap; default False excludes operator-originated trades (reconcile_import is always excluded either way).
why: Introduced 2d32338 2026-06-15 with the per-broker daily-loss caps fix (#727, the frozen-lane incident where a Coinbase-sized global cap froze Robinhood). The default encodes deliberate, documented semantics (Field description + governance.py:1191): manual trades are operator-originated, not lane-generated, so they should not consume the automated lane's budget. This is a policy choice shipped as the correct default, not a dark feature awaiting activation.
risk_if_on: The operator's own manual RH trades would eat the automated lane's daily budget and loss cap → lane starvation/freeze on days he trades alongside the bot — the exact frozen-lane failure class #727 was built to eliminate.

what_it_does: Deployment descriptor (config.py:2139): tells this process the cron scheduler runs in a separate worker — main.py:606 then skips starting the in-process APScheduler, and operator_readiness.py treats scheduler-liveness checks as externally owned.
why: Introduced 99d16cb 2026-04-13 (fix for false stale-pipeline/scheduler-not-running warnings). The effective value is compose-driven: docker-compose.yml:144 sets CHILI_SCHEDULER_RUNS_EXTERNALLY=1 on the app container (paired with CHILI_SCHEDULER_ROLE=none), while dedicated workers carry their own roles. The code default False is correct for bare local dev with no scheduler-worker. Matches the known-evidence posture list.
risk_if_on: It must mirror the real topology, never be 'flipped': set True with no external scheduler-worker running and every cron job (mining, broker sync, reconcilers) silently stops; left False inside the Docker topology and jobs double-run. Verify the in-container binding on the exec worker, not the config default.
